channel.title: CyberNetSec - Patch Management
channel.description: Latest patch management threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: CISA Warns: Critical Adobe AEM Flaw (CVSS 10.0) Actively Exploited
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued an urgent warning for a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Forms, tracked as CVE-2025-54253. The flaw, which carries a perfect 10.0 CVSS score, allows for unauthenticated arb...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Issues 13 Advisories for Critical ICS/OT Vulnerabilities
item.description:
On October 16, 2025, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) released a significant batch of thirteen advisories for vulnerabilities affecting Industrial Control Systems (ICS). These alerts impact widely used Operational Technology (OT) products from major vendors including ...

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Fortinet Discloses High-Severity Authenticated RCE Flaw in FortiOS CLI
item.description:
Fortinet has disclosed a high-severity vulnerability in the command line interface (CLI) of its FortiOS operating system. The flaw could allow an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the underlying system. While a CVE identifier has not yet been assigned and specific affected vers...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Adobe Patches 35+ Flaws, Including Critical RCE Bug in Connect
item.description:
As part of its October 2025 security updates, Adobe has released patches for more than 35 vulnerabilities across a dozen products. The most severe of these is a critical cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Adobe Connect, tracked as CVE-2025-49553, which could lead to arbitrary code execution...

Severity: High

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ivanti Discloses 13 Vulnerabilities in Endpoint Manager, Two High-Severity
item.description:
Ivanti has released a security advisory for its Endpoint Manager (EPM) software, detailing 13 new vulnerabilities. The batch includes two high-severity flaws—one allowing for local privilege escalation and another for remote code execution with user interaction—and eleven medium-severity bugs, many ...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Oracle Issues Emergency Patch for High-Severity EBS Flaw Amid Active Clop Attacks
item.description:
Oracle has released an emergency security patch for a high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2025-61884, in its E-Business Suite (EBS). The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 7.5, allows an unauthenticated, remote attacker to access sensitive data within the Oracle Configurator module. It affects EBS version...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical RCE Flaw in WooCommerce Designer Pro Plugin Puts WordPress Sites at Risk
item.description:
A critical vulnerability, CVE-2025-6439, has been disclosed in the WooCommerce Designer Pro WordPress plugin. The flaw, rated 9.8 out of 10 on the CVSS scale, is a path traversal issue that allows an unauthenticated attacker to delete arbitrary files on the web server. This could lead to complete da...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: WordPress Plugin 'Contest Gallery' Vulnerable to CSV Injection Attacks
item.description:
A medium-severity CSV injection vulnerability, CVE-2025-11254, has been disclosed in the 'Contest Gallery' plugin for WordPress. The flaw affects all versions up to and including 27.0.3. It allows an unauthenticated attacker to embed malicious formulas into data fields that are later exported as a C...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Juniper Networks Patches 220 Flaws, Including Nine Critical Bugs Dating Back Years
item.description:
Juniper Networks has released a massive security update for October 2025, addressing a total of 220 vulnerabilities across its broad portfolio of networking products. The patch bundle includes fixes for nine flaws rated as critical, posing a severe risk of remote code execution or system takeover. A...

Severity: High

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical RCE Flaw (CVE-2025-53967) Patched in Figma AI Tool
item.description:
A high-severity command injection vulnerability, CVE-2025-53967, has been discovered and patched in the 'figma-developer-mcp' Model Context Protocol server, a tool used with the Figma design platform. The flaw, rated with a CVSS score of 7.5, could allow an unauthenticated attacker to achieve remote...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →
